title: Breakwave Dry Bulk Shipping Report 4.25.2023
summary:
Main
in Weekly Shipbuilding Reports 25/04/2023
Volatility declines as a wait-and-see approach takes center stage - The dry bulk market rarely stands still
Source: Breakwave Advisors

Linked asset: 2023-04-25_BreakwaveApril252023Report.pdf
Source asset: reports/hellenic/shipbuilding/pdfs/2023-04-25_BreakwaveApril252023Report.pdf

[Page 1]
BDRY
Dry Bulk Shipping
April 25, 2023
Breakwave Dry Futures Index: 1,826 Baltic Dry Index (spot): 1,517 Short-term Indicators:
↑ 30D: 2.2% ↑ 30D: 1.9% Momentum: Negative
↑ YTD: 76.9% ↑ YTD: 0.1% Sentiment: Neutral
↓ YOY: -38.2% ↓ YOY: -34.2% Fundamentals: Positive
Bi-Weekly Report
• Volatility declines as a wait-and-see approach takes center stage - The dry bulk market rarely stands still, as by
nature it is flooded with volatility, twists and turns, and a highly unpredictable spot market. Yet, the last several
weeks have been unusually calm as spot rates have traded in a rather tight range. Both basins demonstrate signs
of balance, and although demand for transportation has been gradually increasing with higher indicated iron ore
exports from both Brazil and Australia, there have been sufficient ships open to absorb such volumes without any
